title: shinypak
text: shinypak assumes GitHub RStudio (Positron) synced. Read setting gert package website “gert, authentication done automatically using credentials package. package calls local OS credential store also used git command line. Therefore gert automatically pick https credentials safely stored OS keychain.”

title: shinypak
text: authenticating GitHub, typical workflow shinypak : Install load package Find example application chapter follow along (’ll use 02.3_proj-app). Find application look-table list_apps(). can specify regex return table branches matching particular chapter topic: launch app Shiny App-Packages book, can supply name branch launch(): example, 02.3_proj-app branch early chapters Shiny App-Packages (.e., app quite app-package yet): launch() check application already downloaded, download application files folder current working directory, launch app: branch storing app-package, launch() loads package launches application:  ’d prefer download application without launching , can call get_app() function specified branch application downloaded current working directory:  can open new app project supplying open = TRUE argument:  app already downloaded, files updated latest commit branch.
code: install.packages('pak') pak::pak("mjfrigaard/shinypak", force = TRUE) library(shinypak) list_apps(regex = "^02.3")  #>          branch        last_updated #> 5 02.3_proj-app 2025-03-11 13:43:18 list_apps(regex = "proj-app")  #>          branch        last_updated #> 5 02.3_proj-app 2025-03-11 13:43:18 launch(app = "<branch>") launch(app = "02.3_proj-app") ✔ '02.3_proj-app' not in local working directory [59ms] ✔ downloading '02.3_proj-app' [10.7s]  ✔ got '02.3_proj-app' [163ms]          ✔ Launching app with: shiny::shinyAppDir('02.3_proj-app/app.R') get_app(app = "05_roxygen2") get_app(app = "05_roxygen2", open = TRUE)

title: shinypak
text: is_r_package() function useful determining directory contains R package. Consider three folders : folder contains R package, is_r_package() returns TRUE. verbose argument set TRUE, details printed checked: can used quickly determine folder contains R package Shiny app (missing). Consider app folder (DESCRIPTION .Rproj file). tells app fields missing DESCRIPTION .Rproj isn’t configured RStudio’s build tools.
code: path/to/pkg ├── DESCRIPTION └── pkg.Rproj path/to/app ├── DESCRIPTION └── app.Rproj path/to/project └── project.Rproj is_r_package(path = system.file("pkg", package = "shinypak")) #> ✔ '/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/shinypak/pkg' is an R package (DESCRIPTION found, no .Rproj) #> [1] TRUE is_r_package(   path = system.file("pkg", package = "shinypak"),    verbose = TRUE) #> ✔ Package found! #> ✔ Version found! #> ✔ License found! #> ✔ Description found! #> ✔ Title found! #> ✔ Author found! #> ✔ Maintainer found! #> ✔ '/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/shinypak/pkg' is an R package (DESCRIPTION found, no .Rproj) #> [1] TRUE is_r_package(     path = system.file("app", package = "shinypak"),      verbose = TRUE) #> ✖ Package not in DESCRIPTION! #> ✖ Version not in DESCRIPTION! #> ✔ License found! #> ✖ Description not in DESCRIPTION! #> ✔ Title found! #> ✔ Author found! #> ✖ Maintainer not in DESCRIPTION! #> ✖ '/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/shinypak/app' is not an R package (invalid DESCRIPTION, no .Rproj) #> [1] FALSE

what: Clone a Specific Branch of the sap Repository and Optionally Open it — get_app
title: Clone a Specific Branch of the sap Repository and Optionally Open it — get_app
text: Clones specified branch sap GitHub repository current working directory. cleans cloned directory removing .git files, .DS_Store, .Rbuildignore files, optionally renames R project file, optionally opens new IDE session.

title: Clone a Specific Branch of the sap Repository and Optionally Open it — get_app
Clone a Specific Branch of the sap Repository and Optionally Open it — get_app
text: app name branch cloned character string. specified, defaults "main". open logical parameter, set TRUE, function attempt open project directory new session using rstudioapi::openProject. Defaults FALSE.

title: Clone a Specific Branch of the sap Repository and Optionally Open it — get_app
text: Invisible NULL. function called side effects.

title: Clone a Specific Branch of the sap Repository and Optionally Open it — get_app
text: directory branch already exists, deleted re-cloned ensure latest version present. cloning, function performs cleanup operations: Deletes .git directory files. Removes .DS_Store files. Deletes .Rbuildignore files present. renames R project file match name cloned branch , specified, opens project RStudio.

title: Clone a Specific Branch of the sap Repository and Optionally Open it — get_app
text: rstudioapi::openProject function works RStudio Positron. project directory passed directly, .Rproj file required. working directory temporarily changed function's execution reset end.

title: Clone a Specific Branch of the sap Repository and Optionally Open it — get_app

code: is_r_package(      system.file("pkg", package = "shinypak"),      verbose = TRUE) #> ✔ Package found! #> ✔ Version found! #> ✔ License found! #> ✔ Description found! #> ✔ Title found! #> ✔ Author found! #> ✔ Maintainer found! #> ✔ '/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/shinypak/pkg' is an R package (DESCRIPTION found, no .Rproj) #> [1] TRUE is_r_package(      system.file("app", package = "shinypak"),      verbose = TRUE) #> ✖ Package not in DESCRIPTION! #> ✖ Version not in DESCRIPTION! #> ✔ License found! #> ✖ Description not in DESCRIPTION! #> ✔ Title found! #> ✔ Author found! #> ✖ Maintainer not in DESCRIPTION! #> ✖ '/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/shinypak/app' is not an R package (invalid DESCRIPTION, no .Rproj) #> [1] FALSE

what: Launch a Shiny App from the sap Repository — launch
title: Launch a Shiny App from the sap Repository — launch
text: Downloads (needed) launches Shiny application sap GitHub repository. application directory R package, package loaded pkgload::load_all() launching via shiny::runApp(). Plain Shiny apps (DESCRIPTION) launched directly shiny::shinyAppDir().

title: Launch a Shiny App from the sap Repository — launch
Launch a Shiny App from the sap Repository — launch
text: app character string giving branch name application launch (e.g., "02.3_proj-app" "05_roxygen2").

text: Called side effects (launches Shiny app). Returns invisible(NULL).

what: List Branches of the sap Repository — list_apps
title: List Branches of the sap Repository — list_apps
text: function clones 'sap' GitHub repository, extracts list branches along last update time, returns information tibble. repository cloned temporary directory, removed function executes.

title: List Branches of the sap Repository — list_apps
List Branches of the sap Repository — list_apps
text: regex optional character string containing regular expression (regex) filter branch names

title: List Branches of the sap Repository — list_apps
text: data frame two columns: branch (branch name) last_updated (date branch last updated), ordered alphabetically branch name. Branches named HEAD, main, gh-pages excluded.

title: List Branches of the sap Repository — list_apps
text: function operates several steps: saves current working directory revert back later clones 'sap' repository GitHub temporary directory lists branches cloned repository separates branch names source branch_name regex supplied, filters branches matching pattern cleans deleting temporary directory restoring original working directory

Topic lookup table for the sap repository — topic_lookup
text: data frame 40 rows 3 columns: branch Character. branch name (e.g., "05_roxygen2"). part Character. book part branch belongs (e.g., "App-packages", "Tests", "Deploy", "Frameworks", "Shiny & LLMs"). chapter Character. chapter topic (e.g., "Documentation").
